news.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is 'over,' U.S. will 'hit them hard tonight'
news.summary: The president was asked about the ceasefire after an escalation of fighting in the Middle East. The president said the ceasefire was a good step in the right direction. He added that it was not a solution to the problems in the region.

news.title: Fed meeting minutes to show 'family fight' over rates. The squabble could drag on for a while
news.summary: There have been few instances over the past 35 years or so when the Fed has only made one rate move, be it up or down.

news.title: Delta launches 'basic business' fares without lounge access, seat selection
news.summary: Delta is launching basic business and other stripped-down premium classes. The new classes will come with fewer perks, such as a lower price for business class.

news.title: Kentucky Gov. Beshear presses McConnell to provide health update as speculation grows
news.summary: McConnell was admitted to a hospital on June 14. He declined to seek an eighth term following a series of health scares. The former longtime Senate leader was diagnosed with cancer in 2010.

news.title: Waymo starts driverless rides in four more U.S. markets as expansion accelerates
news.summary: Waymo has a big lead in the nascent U.S. robotaxi market, and is opening up in more cities. Waymo has been working with Google to develop a self-driving car.

news.title: Why the stock market’s biggest laggards might be your best defense against a summer selloff
news.summary: Low-volatility stocks are having a moment after a long stretch of underperformance. Low-vol volatility stocks have had a moment in recent months.

news.title: Insurance giant says it’s turning to AI to replace as many as 1,800 jobs
news.summary: Allianz Partners will axe between 1,500 and 1,800 roles from its workforce of close to 23,000. Allianz will axe up to 1,600 roles, or 1,400 of its 23,500-strong workforce.

news.title: ‘I’ll happily wait’: Does delaying Social Security make sense for high earners like me?
news.summary: In many states, Social Security benefits are exempt from state income tax. ‘Social Security is the most secure retirement system in the history of the U.S.,’ says the Social Security Administration.

news.title: ‘Our family is broken beyond repair’: My brother took over my parents’ finances. What can I do?
news.summary: “Without telling me, my brother petitioned for sole guardianship of our mother’s,” she said. “Without knowing what was going on, he petitioned to be sole guardian.”

news.title: I started retirement with $3 million. Now I’m 89 and down to $2 million. What if I get sick?
news.summary: Even if you’re not worried about your normal spending anymore, a long-term-care need is still a problem for any budget.
